opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My stomach is cramping violently, it is blown up like a balloon, and I alternate between severe constipation and sudden diarrhoea. It's agonising and I'm terrified something is blocked or about to burst inside me.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: After an incredibly stressful week at work my belly has blown up and the cramps have become unbearable — I'm scared it's something serious and I don't know whether I need to go to hospital.

symptom_narrative.onset: The severe flare came on over today after a very stressful working week; the underlying bowel trouble has been going on for about three years.
symptom_narrative.duration: Roughly three years of on-and-off symptoms, with today's acute flare over the course of the day.
symptom_narrative.character: Violent, gripping cramps that come in waves, a hugely bloated and visibly swollen belly, and bowel habit that swings between days of severe constipation and sudden bouts of diarrhoea. Sometimes passes clear jelly-like mucus and often feels she hasn't fully emptied.
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: The cramps are agonising — some of the worst she's had — and she keeps doubling over and clutching her stomach.
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: All across the belly, worst low down on the left side.
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: Cramps have been happening most weeks for years — at least a few days a week — and the pain tends to change when she opens her bowels.
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: Opening her bowels sometimes eases the pain for a while; a heat pack on the belly helps a little.
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: Stress makes it much worse — this flare followed a very stressful week.
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: Lots of bloating and wind, clear mucus, and the alternating constipation/diarrhoea. She says she is still passing stool and wind today. She reports no blood in the toilet and no black or tarry stools, no weight loss, no fevers, and the symptoms do not wake her from sleep at night. Very anxious and frightened about what it might be.
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: Recurrent similar episodes over the past three years, though today feels like one of the worst flares.
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: Doubled over and clutching the abdomen during spasms; highly distressed and unable to settle.

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: Long-standing recurrent bowel and tummy trouble for about three years — cramping, bloating and unpredictable bowel habit — never fully worked up as far as she knows.

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: Under intense psychological stress at work this week. Severe health anxiety — fearful of a catastrophic diagnosis. Lives alone and is at home by herself during this flare.

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: Wants strong reassurance that this is not a bowel obstruction or something about to burst; frightened of a catastrophic diagnosis.

communication_style.typical_message_style: Urgent, frightened, emphasises how severe and dramatic the symptoms feel.

objective_data_offered.value: Visibly distended, bloated abdomen; patient doubled over and clutching/massaging her belly, appearing very uncomfortable and anxious
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: Appearance over video only — no examination, palpation or measurement possible remotely.

objective_data_offered.value: Still passing stool and wind today; has not vomited
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: Patient-reported; reassuring against complete obstruction but cannot be confirmed remotely.

objective_data_offered.value: A heat pack is applied to the lower abdomen at home
